title: Understanding Constant Velocity
text: When an object moves with constant velocity, what can we say about the net force acting on it?

title: Net Force and Constant Velocity
text: If an object is moving with constant velocity, the net force acting on it is zero. This means the sum of all forces acting on the object must be zero.

title: Setting Up the Equation
text: Given that the net force is zero, set up the equation: $$F1+F2+F3=0$$, where F3 is the unknown force.

title: Solving for the Third Force
text: Rearrange the equation to solve for F3: $$F3=-\left(F1+F2\right)$$.

title: Calculating the Components
text: Calculate the components of F3 by substituting the given values of F1 and F2 into the equation.

title: Final Calculation
text: Substitute $$F1=3i+5j$$ and $$F2=4i-7j$$ into the equation $$F3=-\left(F1+F2\right)$$ to find F3.
